meta_title: For Freelancers: analytics setup to Solve can't debug code (Advanced Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome can't debug code. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Analytics Setup to Solve Debugging Issues
content.seo_description: Stop guessing why your code fails. Master this advanced analytics setup to gain total observability into your SaaS apps using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: The inability to debug stems from a lack of instrumentation. By implementing event-driven telemetry and structured logging, you transform opaque runtime errors into actionable data. This setup leverages Antigravity for automated observability and Claude Code to inject diagnostic hooks, ensuring you pinpoint failures in production before clients notice.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a production dashboard, the client is pinging you on Slack, and your error logs are nothing more than a graveyard of cryptic stack traces. The code is running, but you are effectively flying blind. In the world of high-stakes freelancing, 'it works on my machine' is a death sentence for your reputation.

True mastery isn't just about writing clean code; it's about building systems that tell you exactly where they bleed. By shifting from reactive troubleshooting to proactive observability, you stop fighting fires and start engineering robust, self-diagnosing SaaS products that keep clients paying and your stress levels low.

steps.title: Define Telemetry Schema
steps.description: Establish a consistent structure for your events. This ensures that every error capture contains context like user ID, session state, and payload history.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a standardized telemetry schema for a Node.js SaaS that captures user context, transaction metadata, and error state in a structured JSON format.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --instruction 'Generate a logger utility that enforces the new telemetry schema across all API routes.'

steps.title: Inject Observability Hooks
steps.description: Programmatically insert diagnostic wrappers around critical business logic to catch edge cases that standard error handlers miss.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a decorator function for TypeScript that wraps async functions, automatically logging entry, exit, and execution time with error handling.
steps.claude_code_command: claude apply --file src/services/payment.ts --pattern 'wrap all public methods with the new observability decorator'

steps.title: Automate Error Classification
steps.description: Use AI to categorize logs into 'Actionable' vs 'Noise', saving you hours of manual log parsing.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an automated script that parses logs and categorizes them by severity, flagging recurring patterns that indicate system bottlenecks.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --file scripts/analyze_logs.py --instruction 'Categorize error logs and summarize the top three recurring issues.'

steps.title: Configure Real-Time Alerts
steps.description: Configure your analytics pipeline to push critical failures directly to your notification stack, bypassing the need to check logs manually.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a configuration for a notification bridge that triggers an alert when error frequency exceeds a 5% threshold in a 1-minute window.

steps.title: Validate Diagnostic Loop
steps.description: Simulate a failure to ensure your new analytics setup captures the correct data needed for immediate resolution.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a test suite that triggers a controlled runtime error and verifies that the telemetry event correctly logs the stack trace and user state.
steps.claude_code_command: claude test --instruction 'Run full diagnostic suite and verify telemetry output matches expectations.'

pro_tips: Always log the 'before' state of a function, not just the error, to reconstruct the exact user journey leading to the crash.
Use unique correlation IDs for every request to trace a single user action across microservices, even if the logs are interleaved.
Implement sampling for high-traffic apps to keep your logging costs low while maintaining statistically significant error data.
Treat your analytics configuration as code; version it in Git to ensure your production observability evolves alongside your features.
Automate the cleanup of logs older than 30 days to avoid storage bloat while maintaining compliance for client audits.
content.next_steps: Once your telemetry is live, set up a weekly 'Observability Review' to analyze recurring error patterns and refactor the most fragile parts of your codebase.
content.conversion_block: Stop guessing and start debugging like a senior engineer. Take control of your production environment today. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
